name: Barbecued Butterflied Leg of Lamb with Mint
recipeInstructions:
1. Lay the meat boned side up. Slash about halfway through the thickest portions, as needed, and pull the meat to make it relatively even.
2. Place the lamb in a 9x13-inch pan. In a 1 1/2 quart pan over medium-high heat, stir vinegar with 1/3 cup mint jelly until boiling. Stir in mint and pour evenly over lamb. Cover and chill for 2 hours or up to a day, turning the meat occasionally.
3. On a barbecue grate, ignite 50-60 charcoal briquets. When the briquets are covered with ash (about 30 minutes), spread them into a single layer and scatter 10-12 more briquets over the coals. Place the grill about 5-6 inches above the coals. Lift the meat onto the grill; reserve the marinade.
4. Turn the meat as needed to brown evenly, basting with the marinade. Cook until the thickest part of the meat is done to your liking (140°F on a thermometer). Thinner sections will be well done.
5. Transfer the lamb to a platter and let rest for 5-10 minutes. Garnish with mint sprigs. Slice the meat thin. Season to taste with mint jelly, salt, and pepper. Serve with a knife and fork or tuck into pocket bread.
